# BAO Score

> BAO Score is a content-analysis tool that measures how the human brain responds to a piece of content — across 6 cognitive dimensions — before it is published. It operationalizes the BAO (Brain Attention Optimization) framework and is powered by the Atenxa Attention Intelligence Platform.

Traditional analytics measure what the platform recorded (views, clicks, impressions). BAO Score measures what the brain registered: cognitive impact, not surface engagement. A user submits a video or URL (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, or a raw video file); Atenxa analyzes it frame by frame, second by second; the user receives a BAO Score (0–100) plus a full attention-architecture report with prioritized fixes, typically within 24 hours.

## The 6 dimensions scored
- Hook Strength: scroll-stop power in the first 300ms — whether the opening frame triggers the brain's threat/reward scanner.
- Curiosity Gap: the brain's reward-anticipation signal — whether the content creates a question the brain needs answered.
- Identity Resonance: niche recognition — whether the brain immediately identifies the content as relevant to its current attentional template.
- Retention Prediction: estimated completion probability based on dopamine reward pacing — where the anticipation engine is likely to drop off.
- Cognitive Ease: mental processing cost per frame — high cognitive load without a reward invites a swipe-away.
- Emotional Density: affective engagement per second — emotion is the brain's primary retention mechanism.

## Core pages
- [Home / Get Your BAO Score](https://baoscore.com): Overview, sample score, and entry point to submit content for analysis.
- [BAO Framework](https://baoframework.com): The underlying discipline and definitions behind BAO Score.
- [Atenxa](https://atenxa.com): The Attention Intelligence Platform that runs the frame-by-frame analysis behind every BAO Score.
